review_text: I bought mine years ago when this model first came out. I noticed the battery wasn't holding a charge anymore so I bought a replacement set for less than ten dollars and did the simple repair myself as per instructions from the battery vendors website. I am due for a set of replacement cutter heads and it will be good as new. The motor runs a well as it did on the first day and that was about ten years ago. When this one finally kicks the bucket and is beyond repair I will not hesitate in buying another Norelco. It is built to last with the normal maintenance of cleaning and lubrication and if you are a bit handy it will last far longer. This model has an adjustment for closeness of cut for those with sensitive skin and a basic flip out trimmer on the back. The head assembly flips open and is also easily removable for cleaning as are each of the cutter heads for a more comprehensive cleaning job. As per the instructions always keep the individual cutters and foil heads paired when cleaning and reassembling as they wear together.
product_title: Norelco 8865XL Spectra Shaving System
product_description: Description: Norelco 8865XL Spectra System Features: Personal Comfort Control Selector Dial adjusts spring and pressure of the floating heads for your specific skin type to maximize comfort and provide our closest shave ever. Two-stage cutting system to shave longer hairs and Holes to shave stubble for a closer, quicker and more comfortable shave. Washable - Rinses clean under faucet for added hygiene and convenience. Contour Control automatically adjusts in multiple directions to hug the unique contours of your face for a closer, smoother shave. Individually Floating Heads move independently for a closer, more comfortable shave. Improved Patented Lift and Cut Technology enhancement to Norelco patented Lift & Cut system. Increased blade width & sharper blade angle lifts whiskers sooner and higher for exceptional closeness. Full-Width Precision Trimmer for grooming mustache and sideburns. Model Key Features: 5 LED battery level, charging, battery full and low indicators. Exquisitely Enhanced Lacquered Finish High Gloss Eggplant. Ultra Silent System low noise motor allows for a quiet shave. Soft Comfort Grip. Travel/Storage Pouch. Electronic On/Off Button. Shaving with or without a cord. Equipped with a dial that varies the spring and pressure of its three floating heads through nine settings to suit skin sensitivity, this corded/cordless electric shaver offers customized shaving comfort. For its Spectra models, Norelco has improved the patented Lift and Cut, below-skin shaving technology of its two-stage cutting system, which provides slots for longer hairs and holes for stubble while the floating heads adjust individually to facial contours. A full-width trimmer pops up for neat work on sideburns and mustaches. On the front is an LED meter that tells how many minutes of shaving time remain when the shaver is operating cordless on its lithium-ion battery. A six-minute quick charge is good for a single shave, while a one-hour full charge provides 100 minutes of cordless shaving. The shaver fits the hand beautifully and goes on the road in a travel pouch while automatically adjusting to worldwide voltage variances. It carries a 60-day, full-satisfaction, money-back guarantee and a two-year warranty against defects. --Fred Brack

review_text: GREAT shaver. Well worth the price! Updated/upgraded from a 10 year old similar model and this one is superb. Used by both husband and wife, the Spectra shaves faces, neck area and legs equally well. Lots of adjustment for closeness. Fits very nicely in the hand. It cleans very easily with the top piece flipping up to the side instead of coming apart separately. Nice feature. Haven't cleaned it under running water yet, our humidity is very high and don't want to worry about it drying completely between uses. QUICK charging and it has a great holder/stand. Would certainly encourage others to buy this product.

review_text: I bought an electric shaver because I'm lazy. I used a Mach 3 for about 4 years and then upgraded to Mach 3 Turbo blades. I got a really close shave (I go with and against the grain) and with the exception of the right and left parts of my chin, there were no signs that facial hair even existed there. But this would take me forever, and the last thing I want to do when I wake up is shave. So I decided to buy an electric shaver. I've had the shaver for about two weeks now. I was not very content with my first coulple shaves, but things improved over time. Pros - Gets very close. The left and right sides of my chin are still difficult to shave, but I get much closer than I used to. - Takes half the time to shave. - There is a little collection compartmant that captures the hair so I can shave in my room while my roommates are taking a shower. - It comes with 9 settings. Cons - Its difficult to shave around the neck. I tend to cut myself a little whenever I try. - The cap was annoying at first becasue it is difficult to tell which side attaches to the front. I ended up drawing an arrow. - Cleaing and drying it is kind of a pain. - Near the end of the battery life, the shaver kind of dies down. Overall, I am satisfied with my purchase. The instruction manuel recommends changing blades after a year (they cost about 20 dollars I think). I think its worth it.

review_summary: Super convenient high performance close shaver
review_text: A gift from my wife a year ago,it has been an inseperable companion since and works like a charm every morning. Began with a sensitive setting and moved to Normal over time. Even though this is my first electric shaver, the transition from the blade was uneventful and before long it became an integral part of the morning routine. Atleast 4 other friends have purchased this model on my recommendation and have enjoyed it. One notch below the top of the line in the spectra model line, it is everything you would need off an electric shaver. Along with the sideburns' trimmer, a nose hair trimmer would have completed the toolset required by a man to manage his facial hair.

review_text: I bought this shaver a while ago, but I wanted to wait at least a month before writing my review, since it's supposed to take about that long before your face is used to an electric razor. This is my first electric, and I was concerned going into it. I looked at the price of good standard blades, though, and saw how quickly an electric razor would pay for itself. I'll never go back. For the first week, the shave was not as close as I had become accustomed with using a blade. After that, though, I've gotten closer and closer shaves by the week. After a little more than a month of using the Spectra, my skin is now smooth even against the grain. I've gotten back to where I was with a blade. The Pros: - Shaving takes easily only half the time as it did with a straight razor. - I can shave any time, any where. With a blade, if I tried to shave more than once per day, I'd cut myself all up into a bloody mess. With this electric, I do notice some discomfort after having shaved a second time in a day, but it gets a nice close shave. A little after shave takes care of the sting, and I'm good to go again. - No mess. I really don't miss having to use shaving cream, even my nifty hot-lather shaving cream. The Cons: - Motor power seems to wind down a bit towards the end of the battery charge. It's not too bad, but it seems to take longer to shave at the end of the charge, and I don't want to charge it early for fear of damaging the battery. - This is really a minor quibble, but I thought I'd mention it: Since this razor rinses clean under tap water, what few bits of hair are still left over are sitting in water over night. Every time I use the razor, for the first minute of shaving, it smells very strongly of old, wet hair. Tips: - Blade users: Apply less pressure than you're used to. I find I get a better shave when the razor is touching the skin firmly, noticibly, but not pressing in at all. - Rinse it out every day, and turn the faucet up high. If you let it go a couple days without rinsing, it's much harder to clean out. It only takes a couple seconds, so why not? - Shave daily. I find that if I let my beard get a little longer the shaver still does a pretty good job. However, there will always be a few hairs that grew too long, curling down, that are not lifted by the razor head. Just shave daily, when you only have stubble, to avoid this. - Get a makeup mirror! These little mirrors are concave, magnifying your face so you can see where you've missed more easily. I mounted mine to the wall, slightly below my chin. Now I can more easily see if I've shaved my neck well.

review_text: The good: Clean, close, comfy, fast shave. Relatively quiet. Good warranty. Gets the tough areas (neck) quickly. Can wet-clean it! The not so good: Old-school charging system and NO smart-charging system (come on Norelco, get with it!!). No rubber-grips. Not enough extras or product for the price. After my bad experience with Braun's expensive, non-performing shaver, I traded it for another Norelco. My previous Norelco lasted me 6 years...why I tried Braun's I'll never know. Once again, my Norelco is giving me a clean, and VERY close shave. The new Spectra system seems to be a hybrid between old reflex and the quadra system. Whatever it is, it works for me. However, if Norelco wants to keep my business in the future they need to start adding better (smarter) functions to the razors and quit with the marketing ploys in their product line...stick to what works. Technology is sufficiently advanced that they can, could and SHOULD start making their shavers with smart-charging systems like Braun!! And put rubber grips on the side of these things!! Do you not realize that we need traction? Also, packaging the same razor body style with or without LED is just silly, choose a style that works best and go with it. Plus, for the price of this thing, they need to throw in a free set of replacement blades or even some lube-oil or something, and not just that cheap travel case. Overall I am happy with the performance of the razor, I just wish Norelco would bring a few more percs to the table for the prices they charge.

review_text: Recently my old electric shaver died, so I had to buy a new one. Since I have tough whiskers and sensitive skin, finding a shaver I can use without hurting the skin on my face and throat is difficult. But, fortunately, I had recently seen some ads on TV about the new Norelco Spectra shavers which have nine adjustable settings to allow them to be customized for closeness of shave and skin sensitivity. I was fascinated by this feature but, quite frankly, I was somewhat put off by the price -- which is significantly greater than I had ever previously paid for a shaver. But, on the other hand, I really wanted a shaver that would not hurt my sensitive skin -- and Norelco shavers have an excellent reputation for quality -- so I took a chance and bought a Spectra. Frankly, using a Spectra is unlike any of the dozen or more shavers I've used over decades of shaving. At the proper setting -- which requires a little (but not too much) trial and error -- it gives a comfortable shave much faster than I've ever experienced before. And the comb/cutter assembly can be cleaned quit simply with running water. Also, it has an indicator to show how much shaving time is left after each shave. If the adjustability, fast shaves, and features of this shaver appeal to you, you will probably find it worth the price.

review_summary: I trust Norelco, but has their quality gone down?
review_text: QUALITY: I have been using my OLD Norelco razor for almost 12 years. Since I excercise the batteries "correctly" (full discharge->full charge) they have lasted up until now. I don't baby it, and I have only had to replace the cutting heads a few times. The old Norelco was built like a tank and has been reliable and DURABLE. I recently started looking for a replacement and figured that if the new razor lasts only half as long, even $$$ is money well spent. Upon opening the box, i was disappointed in several minor ways: 1) Can Norelco PLEASE learn "green" packaging? There is far more blow-molded plastic than necessary. The razor is about 16X the volume of the package. 2) The AC adapter has "grown" compared to the old model. It now has a small wall-wart. The older models used to be a simple plug into the wall. This could be annoying if I lose the special adapter. 3) The travel bag is an obnoxiuos light gray with a hint of lavendar, and larger than necessary. Simply not the most masculine of travel bags. 4) The fairly symmetrical head has a slightly asymmetrical clear plastic cover with no good directionality indicator. This lack of a poke-a-yoke means I'm gonna throw it out soon and go without it. Oh, and it doesn't stay on all that tight, either. 5) After first use, I did a "practice" clean. This worked fine until I gave it a minor shake dry....the head fell off! Easy to put back on (it's a simple metal leaf-spring), but funny nonetheless. C'mon Norelco....this is a "MAN's" product. HOWEVER-> 1+ It definitely gives a much closer shave than my old NORELCO, and quickly! 2+ It holds a lot of charge 3+ (Like my old unit) it has the sensitivity adjustment 4+ Even on "Normal" sensitivity, it doesn't pinch my neck like the older model did. 5+ The trimmer works really well. 6+ The AC adapter cord stretches to 6ft easily. OVERALL RATING: 4 stars. Although it doesn't feel like the tank my old unit was, this is definitely a top notch razor. I would have given it 5 stars except for the clear plastic cover. I plan on getting a good 5 years of daily shaves out of this one. WELL WORTH THE MONEY and i'm glad I bought it.

review_summary: Loved it at first - Now broken
review_text: I've always despised wearing mascara and thought that I had the solution when I bought this 5 months ago. It was shipped in good time and made my lashes look far more feminine than cakey mascara although it did also requires more time than just putting on a few coats with a wand. However, about a month ago the plastic started cracking and I attempted to fix it with superglue to no avail. Now the plastic is so cracked that I have to use my other hand to squeeze the end of the device in order to acheive curl (immensely time consuming). A metal design would have been ideal (though more expensive) yet I suppose $15 wasn't too much to give up for 5 months of lovely lashes.
product_title: Zhen Professional Detail Curler
product_description_filled: Product: Zhen Professional Detail Curler.

review_text: My eyelashes are short but thick and I have the worst time trying to curl my eyelashes with other curlers. I almost always dread it because I get pinched by the other products. After I gave Zhen's curler a try, my eyes look more open with no pain! The smaller curler gets all the eyelashes curled in just seconds. I love it!
product_title: Zhen Professional Detail Curler
product_description_filled: Product: Zhen Professional Detail Curler.

review_text: This curler does a small section of lashes at a time. If you have trouble getting to the very end of your lashes or the inside corner, then this curler is perfect. It takes a little longer to curl all of your lashes but will get even the hardest to reach.
product_title: Zhen Professional Detail Curler
product_description_filled: Product: Zhen Professional Detail Curler.

review_text: It is the best lash curler I ever bought in my life! It is easy to use and you can quickly curl all parts of the lashes in a couple minutes. I am thinking about purchasing some for my sisters.

review_text: Great oil. penitrates skin very nicely
product_title: Organic HobaCare Jojoba Oil 250ml (8.45 oz)
product_description: USDA Certified 100% Organic HobaCare Jojoba HobaCare's Certified Organic Jojoba has been certified by the USDA - the most prestigious organic certification available. This certified organic version features the same expeller-pressed quality as HobaCare's 100% pure, pesticide-free jojoba . This product is technically not an oil but a liquid wax ester and will not become rancid or stain. Unlike oils, this will not clog pores and is non-allergenic. Available in 4.2 oz, 8.4 oz and 33 oz Use caution when laundering linens that have been exposed to oils. Oil products are potentially combustible when exposed to heat in the dryer. Consult dryer manufacturer's guidelines for drying linens that have been exposed to oils. *** During Winter , Jojoba may congeal while in transit . Congealing does not affect the Jojoba. Please remember : Jojoba is not oil . Remove the container from its packaging and leave it at room temperature to thaw out.Please do not use a microwave to speed up the thawing process. After Thawing it , you may notice white wisps in the jojoba or at the bottom of the bottle . They are Phospholipids, an integral constituent of jojoba . Good for the skin! Simply shake the bottle to re-integrate them***

review_summary: Excellent for my dry, sensitive skin
review_text: I have struggled with terrible, constant acne for the last ten years. I tried all OTC products and nothing worked! My skin was always irritated, my face was always red, and I was always in the middle of a breakout. My skin was always dry and peeling (gross!). I finally decided to try more natural products, and it turns out my skin has cleared up! This jojoba oil is the best moisturizer I've ever used. My face is no longer dry and itchy, so I don't scratch it all the time. I no longer have dry bits of skin falling off my face. This has been so much better than any moisturizer I ever used. And my skin doesn't look greasy either. I will definitely keep using this product.

review_summary: Original Gillette M3 Power Razor better than new black handled one
review_text: I first purchased the Gillette M3 Power Razor in 2004 when they were first released. I was just not happy with the Gilette I was using. It was exactly what I was looking for. Wet the face, lather with a small amount of shaving cream, turn on the razor and what a surprise, easier smoother shave. I was pleased. That razor lasted until November 2009 when the green off/on switch popped out and washed down the drain. I stopped at the drug store that night and found the new (improved ???) black handled M3 Razor. The next morning I used it, seemed ok but something was missing that I could not hang a wisker on. In January, it refused to turn on when I tried to use it. I started to replace the battery and noticed that the plastic support that holds the battery in place was crumbling. That is when I started an internet search for the original M3. I found it on amazon decided to order two just to play it safe. at the same time, I read a single review that stated that the razor made the persons neck break out. Started me thinking, I wondered how many newly wiskered youngsters tried shaving without a clue as to what they were doing; after all, there are no instructions on how to shave!! An aside, Gillette has a new super M3 like razor with a 5th blade, costing a lot more money. I thought about it but ordered the Original M3 Power razor anyway. I highly recommend this to anyone who wants a clean shave without breaking the bank over blade replacement. Battery lasts a good three or more months, blades a dozen shaves, depending on beard thickness. Just remember to use water and shave cream or soap, good lather and go for it. For us oldsters, we already know how to shave and know a good Razor when we see it. Gillette, your new improved black handled M3 replacement just does not cut it. I have 5 stars for the Oldie but Goodie!

review_text: I shave once every two days and decided to try M3 Power Nitro (black). It attracted my attention by innovative idea of combining regular razor with electro-vibration, thus making a hybrid. I'm not impressed with it at all! I don't like it for several reasons. Vibration actually is mostly reflected on a handle so I felt uncomfortable because it was making my hand too shaky and I'm 26 years old and not a drinker! It doesn't cause cuts or burns but it felt really annoying to my hand while shaving and even a few minutes later! Due to a better lubricator-strip it glides better, softer than regular Mach3 but shaving performance is about the same. Handle grip is OK but a bit worse than regular Mach3 because it doesn't have fully rubberized handle bottom. It can be used with Mach3 cartridges, I tested and shaved. While shaving I was concerned about not getting wet its battery compartment. I would rather use it as regular Mach3 razor, with vibration turned off, but would NOT buy another! Why pay more for a "fancy" but in fact useless/annoying vibration function if regular Mach3 does practically the same, if not better, job?!

review_summary: The Razor that cuts away the competition
review_text: It is such a simple idea. Take a manual razor and an electric razor and create a hybrid electrical manual razor at a cheap price. Forget about buying any kind of electrical razor or manual razor again. This thing works using a single AAA battery (and is worth investing in some rechargable AAA batteries) which slips into the base by pulling out the end piece. Just press the green button and the razor starts to vibrate. This vibration stimulates the skin and gets those hairs to prick up a little bit so that razor blades (all three baldes, i.e - Mach III/M3) catch better creating a cleaner smoother cut. To be honest the old Mach III model was absolutely great but the M3 Power razor just upgrades that razor into a totally new device. There is simply nothing on the market that remotely resembles this kind of product... nothing. I would not hesitate in sending anyone down to the local drug store with a $20 to pick up this wonderful product that... quite frankly... just making shaving enjoyable again!

review_summary: After experimentation, this razor doesn't live up to the hype.
review_text: I've used the Schick Quattro for about 3 years now and have been happy with it. I thought I would try out this powered M3 razor to see what all the hype was about. Many of my friends said they got the best shave ever using it: No razor burn, bumps, or nicks etc. However, the M3 gave me more razor burn and nicks than any razor I've ever used! Could it have been that I wasn't used to the Mach 3 blades after using a Quattro for years? To rule out blade differences, I subsequently tried the Power Quattro and obtained similar, albeit less drastic, negative results. In particular, both the M3 and Power Quattro really did a number on my neck area. I even had people comment on how abused my neck looked after shaving with these two razors. Both Gillette and Schick assert that vibrations diminish irritation and lead to a superior shave. In my case, I believe that vibrations actually lead to more irritation! My personal experience with these razors, and my cynical nature, lead me to believe that vibrating razors are just another ploy by the shaving industry to get people to buy more product (i.e., aftershave creams and lotions to cover up the mess that such razors create). My advice is to avoid these powered hybrids alltogether and stick with a regular Quattro or Mach 3 razor.

review_text: I had my previous flat iron for many years and never had a problem with it despite the fact that my husband accidently dropped it in the toilet tank. I let it dry out for a few months and it worked like a charm until recently. The plates began to loose their finish and when I was done doing my hair I had a bunch of fly always and loose hairs sticking straight up. It was very noticeable which bothered me. It also became difficult to keep clean so it was time for a new one. The peacock design is what attracted me, I thought it was pretty. This flat iron does a great job. It heats up quickly, it can heat up to 450 but I usually use it at about 400. I am Hispanic and have naturally frizzy hair but it also has a lot of body. I dye my hair it's it's a little on the dry side. This flat iron straightens my hair effortless. My hair glides through the plates easily, I don't hear dragging or crunching sounds and it does not pull my hair. It usually only takes a few pass-throughs until my section of hair is nice, straight and bouncy. The plates are ceramic so they heat up evenly meaning the temperature should be the same on the front tip of the plates as it on the center and back of the plates. The ionic technology reduces fizziness and fly-away hairs. The cord has a 360 turn and is long which I really like and it's very user friendly. The packaging is divine, it comes in a beautiful box with a magnetic closure with a comb and a felt type sleeve for travel. I really like this product but there a few cons. My biggest con would be the cord. It's made up of very stiff plastic material that has a lot of memory and I'm worried that it will eventually crack. The curling iron I had previously had a supple rubber material and never experienced cracking or breakage but it didn't have the 360 swivel cord like this flat iron. The location of the on/off switch and temperature dial is located in between the plates by the hinge. I thought this was an odd place and worry it will eventually be effected by the heat of the plates. All in all I am happy with my purchase. I was granted the opportunity to purchase this product at a discounted rate in exchange for my honest review. review_text: This iron is mediocre in performance and quality. The iron is very heavy to hold and has a semi long heat up time. This isn't an iron I'd honestly recommend and the price the company charges full price is outrageous for the quality of this iron. My hair texture is 3C/4A and it is relaxer free. It requires high heats to lay flat. All of my irons is set to 450 degrees when in use. Before any flat ironing can begin I start with clean freshly shampooed and conditioned hair. I then blow dry, prestreching it so that it is easier to iron out. I use a blow out cream which also protects my hair from heat damage and a vented brush when blow drying. After I'll apply a argon oil serum and a frizz busting serum to my hair. These oils aide in getting my hair to lay flat with out any frizz. Iron snagged my hair upon first use. I thought that maybe it was user error but it wasn't as the second time I ran the iron across a different section of hair it was snagged. I also had issues with my iron staying on. The placement of the on and off switch makes it very easy to turn this iron on and off while in use. The iron didn't even stay plugged in when I was using it. I was constantly giggling the cord to get it to turn back in. This iron doesn't glide down a section of hair well. It is very choppy thus leading to the snagged hair I spoke of earlier. The iron does swivel while in use making it easy to use. It goes up to 450 degrees but it's very difficult to maintain any temperature selected on this iron. I don't recommend this iron simply due to the fact it snags at hair. It does work to smooth down hair but it doesn't work well as it cannot guide down smoothly. The only good thing about this iron is the fact it is very attractive to look at. I'm happy to say that I didn't pay full price as I received this for a promotional price. Had I paid full price I would have promptly returned this iron. *** In exchange for my unbiased and honest review I received this iron for a promotional price.

review_summary: Great styling brush and quality that does exactly what I wanted!!!
review_text: I love this styling brush!!! I'm 58 and have medium length, thick, fine hair that has a bit of natural body but not like a light perm or anything, and I've always lost a lot of hairs when washing and styling my hair. I was concerned about my hair routine wrecking my hair as I got older and for about 6 years now I've changed my hair care products, styling tools, hair clips, etc., to help promote healthier hair and reduce hair loss and breakage. A few years ago I found a boars hair round styling brush that worked very well. But when it broke after a few years, I decided to look for a boars hair heated styling brush. I've used heated styling brushes before and for the most part love them. The ones with rotating bristles are pinch points and some tangle my hair worse than others. Definitely, you have to use heated styling brushes differently - you can't use them like a curling iron and wind all the way from the ends to the roots or you will surely have a tangled mess. Been there, done that - yikes! The way I've found that works best is to dry and lift the roots and then work your way to the ends. The L'Azzio takes a minute or two to get up to temperature and on high it isn't an extreme heat, like others I've tried are. For a styling product, this is actually what you want so you don't fry your hair with heat while your actually trying to style it. What I do first is blow dry my hair until it's damp, then use the L'Azzio to heat style it in sections, and then set the top with velcro rollers and hair spray while I finish getting dressed (if I have time). This boars hair brush is well made and helps reduce tangles while I style my hair. And it adds body to the roots and overall gives my hair a fluffed up look that holds all day. I'm so happy I finally found this. I'm planning to order another one as a spare...its that good!

product_title: Konjac Pet Care Sponge for Dogs & Cats - Gently Cleanses and Removes Tear Stains and Poodle Eye - All Natural and Cruelty Free
product_description:
How to use your pet sponge

Thoroughly hydrate sponge before using on your pet

Gently wipe away gunk and dirt in affected area

Rinse sponge completely after each use. Hang dry with attached string

Compost when it wears out. It's completely biodegradable.

Konjac sponges are an all-natural, soft and gentle skincare tool. Use as part of your pets heath care and grooming. Works well even for pets very sensitive skin or skin allergies. Konjac can help hydrate the skin and balance pH.

Konjac sponges are made entirely from the root of the Konjac plant which is native to Korea, Japan, China and southeast Asia. They contains fibers that are by nature alkaline, and thus balances the skins natural acidity.

These fibers are packed with nutrients including iron, phosphorus, copper, zinc, vitamins A, E and D, B vitamins 1, 2, 6 and 12, vitamin C, and folic acid.

Flat-faced dogs like pugs, Pekingese, boxers, and bulldogs can be more prone to eye discharge than other breeds because their flatter faces often mean shallower eye sockets and protruding eyes. Called brachycephalic breeds, dogs with more prominent eyes may have tear drainage problems

Breeds with loose facial skin, like bloodhounds, cocker spaniels, beagles, Saint Bernards, and some terriers, can also benefit from cleaning around the folds of skin on the face, or drooling around the mouth

review_text: I have two the cutest, smartest little Maltese-Shitzu; they were my babies even before my son was born, therefore I am very over protective of them. My Shaka (the boy) always seems to get cold in his eyes, but I don't want to hurt or irritate such a sensitive area. This product worked wonders. I just soaked it as directed, held over the eyes for a minute or two to allow for softening and viola he was back to his rambunctious self; his nickname is Diego, as he loves to explore. Thank you Konjac Pet Care!

review_summary: Great gentle facial tool for dogs that don't like having faces washed. May be lightening tear stains too--will update review.
review_text: This is an interesting way to market a Konjac Sponge, i.e. for your pets. I have to say though, it's pretty brilliant. I've only worked on my white chihuahua-blend's tear stains once with the All About Health Network Charcoal konjac sponge, but it did seem like it lightened his tear stains a bit. They're pretty significant now since he doesn't like his face washed. What was most important to me is that he actually seemed to like me cleaning his face with the sponge. It's very soft once hydrated, and the shape meant I could be careful not to get too near to his eyes. I also have a white frenchie who absolutely HATES having her face washed, but needs to have the folds of her face cleaned regularly. I'm going to order a second one because this has made that process much easier as well. The only reason I gave it 4 stars instead of 5 is that I don't yet know if it can remove tear stains, but given how hard they are to get rid of I somehow doubt it. I'll update the review if it works well over time though! Please note I most likely purchased this at a discount in exchange for an honest and unbiased review. In order to save time, I do as much of my shopping online as is humanly possible. I rely heavily on other customers reviews when making purchasing decisions, so as such, I take the honest and unbiased bit very seriously. While I enjoy testing products, I don't hesitate to write a critical review or rate items low when warranted and all opinions and words are my own. If you find the review helpful it would be great if you would click the "yes" button below. Thanks!

review_text: I have mixed emotions about this wrap first off let me say it works. Second, I was one of the "lucky" few that experienced what is known as "toxic bloat"-- an awful side effect that causes severe bloating and gas. The second wrap was much better and I saw lots of results. From what I've read those that have "toxic bloat" are those with lots of toxins... yuck! Glad I could get rid of those. I would suggest doing this for the first time when you don't have a lot to do for a couple of days (weekend), so that if you happen to be one that experiences side effects you have time to recover. I love that the wrap was easy to use- simply unwrap apply and plastic wrap into place. I like that I could continue with my daily activities while workin' on my bod, lol. And, I love that I could do it at home! The wrap had a pleasant smell and left my skin feeling refreshed (not sticky). If you're looking for a quick fix- ie, getting skinny without exercising- this is not it. This is something that should be done in addition to a healthy diet and exercise. I received this product complimentary to test and give honest feedback based on actual use.

review_text: I don't use this everyday, but I've had this for at least 2 years, and it still works great. I tried more expensive models with the short clip to hold the ends of your hair, and I actually prefer this 1. The black rotating part prevents you from getting the wand right up against your scalp, ears, neck, etc. The timer/beep is really helpful. I like that it guides your hair around the barrel, so you don't have to get your fingers close. It smooths the lock of hair as it wraps, which I especially like. I also like that you can use the barrel sideways close to your scalp to create more volume at your roots. I have naturally wavy, fine, medium textured hair, and this breaks the natural texture without creating fried hair. I normally run it around 325-350 with a heat protecting/styling spray, and it still makes pretty tight curls at the lower heat. I let it sit in ringlets while I finish my makeup and get dressed, then I comb through it with my fingers. Hair spray on the roots and finishing spray on the length, and it lasts all day. I've even slept on the curls, brushed my hair the next day, then sprayed my hair, and the curls came right back.

review_text: The curls stay in for more than one day. I love this product.

product_description_filled: Product: Kiss Professional InstaWave Automatic Curler (Purple).

review_text: My favorite curling tool ever - and I've had lots, including hot rollers, curling irons with and without clamps, curling brushes, and several sizes of marcel curling irons (my favorite up until now). I can curl my hair faster with this tool than any of the others (6 to 10 minutes); it heats up in just seconds; the curls are very well formed and long lasting, and my hair is really shiny. My hair comes about to my bra strap, so long, but not super long. I've never had a problem with it tangling. I found it easy to learn to use - I was very comfortable with it by about the third time I used it. Tips: Section off your hair. Grab and comb through the section you want to curl. I hold the curler up next to the section and press the button to rotate it, to confirm that I've got it going in the right direction, and to get it to the point where the vertical tab at the base of the heated section is facing toward me. I slide the section of hair between the curling rod and that tab, about inch from the roots, then press the button to wind the hair onto the rod. I find that doing it that way gets me the most even curl, and ensures the curl starts close to my roots, as opposed to starting part way down, or being significantly tighter at the ends than the roots. After I've done my whole head, I go do something else for at least 10 minutes, then brush it out. The curls are strong enough to take brushing out with out getting frizzy or totally pulling the curl out.

review_text: Very nice makeup blender. This blender was very soft and a perfect size to blend foundations and for contouring. I'm relatively new to contouring but I am getting the hang of it. What I have learned is that the blender is key to success. Without a good blender that stipples the makeup well, you just end up looking like a mess. This blender definitely stepped up to the plate and did a fantastic job blending in the contouring. It didn't soak up the makeup like some sponges do, wasting what is usually pretty expensive product. Just make the sponge a little moist and gently tap repeatedly and before you know it you have a nice smooth blend. Not all sponge blender are made alike, you want a high quality blender and this one form Derma fits the bill. I purchased this product at a discounted price in exchange for my honest and unbiased review.

review_text: I found that this blender works at its best when wet. It expands to almost double its size when wet and foundation/cream makeup does not soak into it, which is a really great thing. When I applied my foundation, it went on smooth. No spots that had more foundation than the other. It did not stick to the blender or make it hard to blend with my concealer, as it got into the smallest crevice near my eye with the point of the blender. So impressed with that. It also applies BB cream perfectly across the face. The trick with using the blender when wet is to kind of pat the foundation/concealer/BB cream onto the face, as opposed to wiping. I am very happy with this blender and recommend to all. I received this product at a discounted price in exchange for an honest and unbiased review.

review_text: I admit I'm not much of a makeup user, but my 16 year old daughter is. She loves this blender. The size is easy for her to hold and with it being smaller it's easier for her to get near her eyes. It's not so small that it takes longer to blend larger areas though. It doesn't soak up as much of the makeup, which is a plus for me as it means less waste. It blends very well without streaking. The material also makes it easier to clean which is always her biggest complaint with other sponges. I can't wait to see if the material lends to a longer life expectancy as most of the spongier ones wear out quicker with her frequent use. review_summary: SO happy with this brush! Perfect for very sensitive skin!
review_text: I'm so happy I took a chance and purchased this cleansing brush because I LOVE it!! I have a clarisonic mia brush and I can't hardly use it because it irritates the crap out of my super sensitive skin. I heard so many people raving about the clarisonic and thought it would be worth the investment, but the scrubbing was just to harsh on my face. My husband (who does not have sensitive skin) loves the clarisonic and didn't feel that it was too harsh at all so I let him have my clarisonic (with a new brush head of course) and decided I'd try out one of these vibrating, silicone brushes instead since they seem like they'd be much more gentle on my skin. I looked at the Foreo Luna but really didn't want to invest that much money into another cleansing brush after having just bought the clarisonic. That is when I saw this Letigo Sonic Facial brush here on Amazon and thought it look very similar to the Luna and for only $15. Let me just say, it did not disappoint. It has two sides, one for cleansing and one for massaging. It also comes with a usb charger and has up and down settings so that you can change the intensity of the vibration. I also think it looks to be better than the Foreo Luna because of the slightly angled design that it has. The slight bend in it makes it fit perfectly and comfortably into your hand. My face feels so clean after using it and isn't irritated at all. In fact, it feels amazing. I would totally recommend this brush to anyone and to anyone reading this, if you are debating on whether to get it, BUY IT NOW. It is worth every penny.

review_text: Great little cleanser! I had been looking for a cleanser system that was gentle enough for my sensitive skin, I have tried different brands of brushes from cheap to more expensive ones and even using the sensitive or gentle attachments it still irritated my face. I didn't want to spend over $100.00 again for something that wouldnt work, and I was pleasantly surprised at how well this worked with my skin type. This was very affordable, less than what you would spend buying a brush head replacement, it is made of silicone so you just rinse it when you are done and don't have to worry about bacteria. It comes with a USB charging cord, which you can use on your computer, laptop, or phone charger (if it uses USB) I used my iPhone charger and it had a full charge pretty fast. I couldn't wait to give it a try, so I put it in the charger right away, and while I applied cleanser to my face it had already enough charge to be used for the first time. You can adjust the speed of the pulses which allows you the option of cleaning/exfoliation/massage. I received this product at a discounted price in exchange for my honest review, this doesn't affect my opinion at all. I have used it to clean my face since I received it twice a day, in the morning and evening, it has worked great, no irritation at all, and I like the softness of my face after I use it, the vibration on the skin is like having a massage, it is very relaxing. It is waterproofed and I have used it in the shower with no problems. The only thing that I would recommend is to be careful with the little plug where you insert the charger, it is thin, and if you use too much force when you open it, it might rip apart. Thank you for reading, and I hope the review and the pictures are helpful.

review_summary: You get what you pay for. (UPDATE: I would recommend)
review_text: Well... Alright. So most of the brushes are okay. They feel/look cheap, but I wasn't too worried about that. However, because they were so cheaply made, 4 of the black tips weren't even glued on, one was glued but only half way, 3 of the brushes had bristles sticking out so those had to be pulled/trimmed, and 3 brushes were so stiff that it rendered them useless. I will be using them, but most likely not for long. I tried cleaning the bristles several times, but they're just so hard and sticky. I can still bend the bristles completely down and it sticks that way... I don't know how else to wash the glue away. Hot water and dish soap didn't do it, so what else could? Any tips would be appreciated. The big fluff brush is a different color than pictured, but that's not a big deal. All in all... It *was* a let down, but I'm not too mad about it because it was so cheap. I personally wouldn't recommend these because there have been incidences where people have gotten far worse than what I received, but that's a gamble you'd have to make. I'll update within a few months to see how these hold up. They are short (which I like). They're about the same size as the EcoTools brush sets from drug stores. UPDATE: Okay, it's a toss-up between 3 and 4 stars. 4 stars because they HAVE held up (even the semi-broken ones), but 3 stars because there just isn't any reason to distribute unfinished products. Anyway, they apply makeup nicely. They're easy to use, easy to clean, and I probably prefer these over any other brushes I have used within this price range. For the price, I say go for it. If you don't like them, at least they weren't expensive.
